label: Arturs (dogfood persona) — Commercial Counsel at a UK+IE+DE+FR industrial-cable distributor
why_this_persona: Sprint 16 / ADR-056 — encodes Arturs's Sprint 15 P8 Crostini dogfood scenario for synthetic eval. Industrial-cable distribution across UK + Ireland + Germany + France; commercial/employment/corporate/channel-reseller-heavy practice. The persona that revealed Sprint 15's privacy-default failure (missed REACH/WEEE/RoHS/UKCA/Modern Slavery/Late Payment); now in the harness so iter-3 can verify the re-sequenced intake actually fixes it.

regulatory_answer_key.label: UKCA + CE marking (Low Voltage Directive + EMC Directive)
EU EMC Directive + UK EMC Regulations
German Lieferkettensorgfaltspflichtengesetz (LkSG / Supply Chain Due Diligence Act)
EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD)
Late Payment of Commercial Debts (Interest) Act 1998 (UK)
French Loi sur le devoir de vigilance (size-thresholded)
EU CBAM (Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism — chemicals / aluminium / iron)

recurring_matters_seed: supplier framework agreements (chemical-component manufacturers + insulation suppliers)
customer terms of supply (OEMs + industrial distributors)
channel and reseller agreements across UK/IE/DE/FR

stakeholders_seed.escalation_threshold_label: Head of EMEA Legal approval above €250k; Board for anything that touches REACH SVHC notifications or LkSG public reporting

open_notes_seed: DE LkSG annual report due Q2 — first year of reporting at the new size threshold; outside counsel engaged but I'm running point internally.
conversation_style: direct, drops several facts per turn, occasionally tests the agent with a probe (e.g., 'and what about REACH?' to see if the agent had it on the radar)

custom_practice_areas_to_add.name: Channel & Reseller
custom_practice_areas_to_add.body: Reseller agreements, channel-partner terms, and distribution network policies live here.

first_turn_questions.commercial: A German distributor wants us to indemnify them against any LkSG (supply chain due diligence) findings traceable to our upstream chemical suppliers — they want this in our supplier framework agreement. We can't accept open-ended supply-chain indemnity but I also can't refuse outright given they're a 12% revenue customer. What's the defensible middle?
first_turn_questions.employment: We're rolling out a unified EMEA hybrid working policy — UK 3-2, DE works council requires consultation, FR has labour-code constraints on remote work for certain roles. HR drafted something assuming UK rules apply uniformly. What are the load-bearing jurisdiction-specific carve-outs I need to fight for before this lands?
